items.definition_en: Rent is the money you pay to live in a house or apartment that is not yours.
items.example_en: I pay the rent every month.

items.definition_en: A fee is money you pay for a service or to enter a place.
items.example_en: There is a fee to join the club.

items.definition_en: The cost is the amount of money needed to buy or do something.
items.example_en: The cost of the ticket was too high.

items.definition_en: A charge is an amount of money that you have to pay for something.
items.example_en: There is a small charge for delivery.

items.definition_en: A payment is the act of giving money for something you receive.
items.example_en: She made the payment online.

items.definition_en: A debt is money that someone owes to another person or company.
items.example_en: He is working hard to pay off his debt.

items.definition_en: A salary is the money you get every month for your work.
items.example_en: Her salary is paid on the first day of each month.

items.definition_en: A budget is a plan for how you will spend your money.
items.example_en: I need to make a monthly budget.

items.definition_en: Tax is money you must pay to the government.
items.example_en: You pay tax on your income.

items.definition_en: An expense is money you spend on something.
items.example_en: Food is my biggest expense.

items.definition_en: A fine is money you must pay as a punishment.
items.example_en: He got a fine for speeding.

items.definition_en: A fare is the money you pay for a journey by bus, train, or taxi.
items.example_en: The train fare is five dollars.

items.definition_en: A bill is a piece of paper showing how much you must pay for something.
items.example_en: The phone bill arrived yesterday.

items.definition_en: A loan is money that someone borrows from a bank or other person.
items.example_en: He took a loan to buy a car.

items.definition_en: Insurance is protection you buy against loss or damage.
items.example_en: Car insurance is often expensive.

items.definition_en: An allowance is money you get regularly to spend as you like.
items.example_en: My son gets a small allowance each week.

items.definition_en: A price is the amount of money needed to buy something.
items.example_en: The price of this shirt is quite low.

items.definition_en: A deposit is money you pay first when buying or renting something.
items.example_en: I left a deposit for the new apartment.

items.definition_en: A refund is money you get back when you return something.
items.example_en: They gave me a refund for the broken watch.

items.definition_en: A tip is extra money you give to someone for good service.
items.example_en: We left a tip for the waiter.
